summary: Tested 14 cases (5 new SOTA + 9 regression) on OmniLegal v4 backend at https://rag-source-refined.preview.emergentagent.com. 11/14 PASSED, 3 FAILED. PASSED: adversarial/find (kill_thesis + counter_precedents + 30+ candidates), drift/analyze (verdict=stable, total_hits>=100, 7 buckets), sentinel/scan (rules_checked=17, 4+ confirmed findings, risk>0.3, high>=3), sentinel/rules (17 rules with full schema), stress/test (8 hypotheticals, 6+ flaws, coverage_distribution all keys), redteam (3 weak_points + 5 counter_arguments), doctrine/track (8 milestones — fix verified, was 0), graph/build (15 nodes — fix verified, was 1), live/search 4 sources (>=6 hits across IK/CL/EUR-Lex/HUDOC), reading/annotate (terms+citations), reports POST+GET (id+share_token roundtrip), diff_chunks/counts/impact present. FAILED: (1) arbitrage/scan returns 502 from kubernetes ingress at exactly 60s — backend actually returns 200 (confirmed in supervisor logs `POST /api/arbitrage/scan HTTP/1.1" 200 OK`) but the upstream proxy enforces a ~60s timeout and the endpoint takes 70-120s due to CL 429s + EUR-Lex 406s + Claude waterfall. (2) diff/compare response is missing the required `used_model` field (returns left_label/right_label/diff_chunks/metrics/impact/counts but no used_model/model). (3) voice/verify_chunk returns claims=[] because the sentence splitter cuts 'In Roe v. Wade...' at 'v.' producing two fragments ('In Roe v.' / 'Wade the court held...') so the citation regex never matches the full case name — verdict comes back as 'no_citations' and trust_score=0.

critical.issue: Kubernetes ingress 502 after 60.0s. Backend log confirms it eventually returns 200 OK (~85-120s) but preview ingress drops the connection at 60s. Root cause stack: (a) CourtListener returns 429 Too Many Requests on every parallel jurisdiction lookup, forcing slow retries/fallbacks; (b) EUR-Lex SPARQL endpoint returns 406 Unacceptable on every call (Accept header rejected) so all EU lookups also slow-fail; (c) Claude/Gemini waterfall adds 20-40s per LLM round-trip. Fix options: shrink the per-jurisdiction parallel fan-out, set tighter per-source HTTP timeouts (5s) so failures fail fast, or move to a polling/job pattern.

Response is missing the required `used_model` field that the spec mandates. Confirmed via curl: keys returned = ['left_label','right_label','left_text','right_text','diff_chunks','metrics','impact','counts']. The diff content itself is good (impact.summary present, 1 reworded chunk, full counts), but downstream consumers expecting used_model will break.

Returns claims=[] for input 'In Roe v. Wade the court held abortion was protected.' because the sentence splitter treats the period after 'v' as a sentence boundary. The text is split into ['In Roe v.', 'Wade the court held abortion was protected.'] and the citation regex 'Roe v. Wade' never matches in either half. summary_counts.no_citations=2, trust_score=0.0, verdict='no_citations'. Need to harden the splitter to recognise legal abbreviations ('v.', 'vs.', 'No.', 'Inc.', 'Ltd.') as non-terminal.

minor.issue: Emergent LLM key budget exhausted: backend logs show repeated `Emergent LLM call failed: Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.4239, Max budget: 0.4`. The waterfall correctly falls through to Gemini/Groq, but every Claude-primary call now pays a ~1-2s penalty before failover and quality degrades. Top up EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget.

Every EUR-Lex SPARQL call returns 'HTTP Error 406: Unacceptable'. The Accept query-string param 'application/sparql-results+json' is being rejected (or the server requires it as a header instead of query string). EUR-Lex is therefore always falling back to curated GDPR-only results — review request explicitly flagged this should be fixed. Try sending Accept as an HTTP header instead of `&format=...` query parameter, or use `application/json` as the Accept value.

CourtListener v4 returns 429 Too Many Requests on every parallel jurisdiction call during arbitrage. Concurrent fan-out exceeds the ~5 RPS limit. Add a semaphore (asyncio.Semaphore(3)) or sequential dispatch for CL specifically.

action_items: Fix /api/arbitrage/scan timeout — endpoint exceeds 60s ingress limit; reduce CL fan-out, add per-source 5s timeouts, or convert to async job + poll.
Add `used_model` field to /api/diff/compare response (currently absent — required by spec).
Fix /api/voice/verify_chunk sentence splitter — must not split on period inside legal abbreviations like 'v.', 'vs.', 'Inc.', 'Ltd.', 'No.' otherwise case citations are never detected.

Replace EUR-Lex SPARQL Accept query-string param with HTTP Accept header (currently every call returns 406). Today every EUR-Lex result is curated-fallback, undermining the 'real SPARQL' claim in the review request.
Top up EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget (currently exceeded 0.4 cap — every Claude primary call fails through to Gemini).
Throttle CourtListener concurrency (asyncio.Semaphore(3) or sequential) to avoid 429s during multi-jurisdiction scans.
critical_code_review_comments: src/services/live_authority_service.py — EUR-Lex SPARQL is broken (406 on every call) yet the service silently falls back to curated GDPR list. Add a metric/log when fallback is used so callers can tell live vs cached. Also marks the entire 'EUR-Lex now uses real SPARQL' claim in the review request as currently false in production.

src/services/arbitrage_service.py — fan-out across N jurisdictions × M sources without bounded concurrency. CL rate-limits at ~5 RPS but the service spawns ≥10 parallel CL requests, causing 429 cascades. Add asyncio.Semaphore per provider.

src/services/voice_service.py (or wherever sentence splitting happens) — naive period-based sentence splitting breaks every legal citation containing 'v.'. Use a legal-aware splitter or pre-replace 'v.', 'vs.', 'Inc.', 'Ltd.', 'No.', 'Cir.', 'Fed.', 'U.S.' with placeholders before splitting.

src/api_router_v3.py /diff/compare — response model omits `used_model` while every other LLM endpoint (research, council, advocacy) includes it. Pydantic response_model would have caught this. Add it.

Emergent LLM waterfall — when Anthropic primary fails on budget, every endpoint now pays a ~1-2s penalty per call. Cache the budget-exceeded state for ~60s and skip Anthropic until reset to reduce latency.
seed_data_creation: None — Qdrant corpus empty as expected; all tests hit live registries.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_omnilegal_v4_sota.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/v4_sota_results.xml. Suite takes ~5 min. The 5 SOTA endpoints (adversarial/drift/sentinel/stress) all PASS in under 60s; arbitrage is the only SOTA failure (gateway timeout). Regression tests for doctrine (was 0 milestones) and graph (was 1 node) are now confirmed FIXED — doctrine returns 8 milestones, graph returns 15 nodes. Live search returns >=6 hits across 4 sources but EUR-Lex hits are all curated-fallback (not real SPARQL — flagged in minor issues). Diff impact/counts/diff_chunks all good; only `used_model` missing. Voice verify works for non-'v.' citations — the bug is specifically the sentence splitter; try a different test sentence like 'Section 124A IPC criminalises sedition.' to confirm the verifier works otherwise.

rca of the issue.arbitrage_502: Preview ingress times out at 60s. Backend completes the work in 70-120s and returns 200 (verified via supervisor backend log: `INFO: 127.0.0.1:60682 - POST /api/arbitrage/scan HTTP/1.1 200 OK` ~115s after request). Slowness compounded by EUR-Lex 406, CL 429, Claude budget-exceeded fallback. Fix at the application layer: reduce concurrent CL calls, add 5s per-source HTTP timeout, and short-circuit when EUR-Lex 406s.
rca of the issue.diff_used_model_missing: The /api/diff/compare handler in api_router_v3.py returns the raw service dict which never includes the LLM model name. Other endpoints attach used_model from the waterfall response. Add `used_model = waterfall_result.model` to the diff response.
rca of the issue.voice_no_claims: src/services/voice_service.py (sentence splitter) splits 'In Roe v. Wade the court held...' on the period after 'v' yielding ['In Roe v.', 'Wade the court held abortion was protected.']. The citation regex pattern '[A-Z]\w+ v\. [A-Z]\w+' never matches across the split boundary. Replace 'v.', 'vs.', 'Inc.', 'Ltd.', 'No.', 'U.S.', 'Cir.', 'Fed.' with sentinels before splitting and restore them after, or use a legal-aware sentence segmenter (blackstone, eyecite, or regex with negative lookbehind for these abbreviations).
